title: When All Doors Are Closed
body:
When you’re in pain, ask yourself:
Is your problem bigger than the one in the belly of a fish?
Bigger than the one left in a dark well?
Bigger than the one thrown into fire?
Bigger than the one with an army behind and the sea ahead?

No. They are not.

Your problems may be hard, but you can still imagine a way out.
But what does science say about surviving inside a fish, a well, or the sea?

A doctor once said, and he was right:
True faith is when you can’t see the way, but you’re still at peace, knowing He will handle it.

He, above whom there is nothing.
He, by whose will everything happens.

One Name: *Musabbib-ul-Asbaab* — The One who creates the causes.
For us, rain needs clouds. But He is the One who made that rule.

Have faith. He will take care.
He got you through those dark, shaking nights. He will get you through what comes next too.
He hears you. He sees you. He knows everything.

What He decreed will happen.
What He didn’t decree will never happen.

*So what do we do? Where do we go? What decision is right?*
These questions tear us apart.

Just pray.
Keep trying while staying within Allah’s limits.
Don’t lose hope.

Whether it goes your way or not, the destination is already set by Him.
Want to change it? Pray. If He wills, He will. If not, His decision is best.

In this short life, even getting everything you want won’t be enough.
Be content with how He keeps you.

Maybe this all sounds like “just words.”
But what feels like just words, aren’t always just words.

I’m with you. I pray for you.
excerpt: A heartfelt reminder about faith in hard times. It compares our struggles to the trials of the Prophets and says no hardship is bigger than theirs. The core message: trust Allah even when you see no way out. He is _Musabbib-ul-Asbaab_ — The Creator of causes. Keep praying, keep trying, and accept that His plan is always better than ours.

title: Light Finds a Way
caption: Even when the day is ending, the sun still finds gaps to shine through.
